tool.name: parse_api_spec
tool.description:
Parse an OpenAPI/Swagger specification to extract a security-prioritized test plan.
Supports OpenAPI 3.x and Swagger 2.x (JSON and YAML). Extracts all endpoints, parameters, auth schemes, and generates a risk-scored test plan prioritizing injectable parameters, IDOR candidates, and auth endpoints.
Use this when the target has an API spec (often at /openapi.json, /swagger.json, /api-docs, or /v2/api-docs).

parameter.name: spec_content
parameter.description: The full OpenAPI/Swagger spec content (JSON or YAML string). Fetch it first via terminal_execute (curl) or browser_action.

parameter.name: base_url
parameter.description: Override the base URL from the spec. Useful when the spec has a relative or incorrect server URL.

returns.description: Response containing: - spec_version: detected OpenAPI/Swagger version - base_url: resolved base URL - total_endpoints: count of endpoints - auth_schemes: list of auth mechanisms - summary: attack surface summary (injectable params, IDOR candidates, etc.) - test_plan: top 50 endpoints sorted by risk score - all_endpoints: complete endpoint list

tool.notes:
Workflow: 1. Discover the API spec URL (try /openapi.json, /swagger.json, /api-docs, /v2/api-docs) 2. Fetch the spec via terminal_execute: curl -s https://target.com/openapi.json 3. Pass the content to parse_api_spec 4. Use the test_plan to systematically test endpoints by risk score 5. Focus on: injectable parameters, IDOR candidates, auth bypass, file upload endpoints
The risk score considers: - HTTP method risk (DELETE > PUT/PATCH > POST > GET) - Injectable parameter names (id, user_id, email, query, redirect, etc.) - Request body presence - Path parameters (IDOR potential) - Auth requirements (bypass testing) - File/upload/admin related paths
